questions.question: What is the primary function of the Self-Attention mechanism in Transformers?

options.A: To handle the long-range dependencies in the input data
options.B: To scale down the input data
options.C: To convert input data to vectors

questions.explanation: The Self-Attention mechanism enables Transformers to model long-range dependencies in the input data, allowing it to capture complex interactions between different elements of the input sequence.

questions.question: In the Transformer architecture, what is the purpose of the Feed Forward Neural Network (FFNN)?

options.A: To add non-linearity in the model
options.C: To output the final probability distribution of the output sequence

questions.explanation: The FFNN adds non-linearity to the model, allowing it to learn complex relationships between the input elements. This is essential for modeling the output sequence.

questions.question: What is the name of the sub-layer that performs the self-attention mechanism in the Transformer architecture?
questions.explanation: The Multi-Head Attention is the sub-layer that performs the self-attention mechanism, allowing the model to capture complex interactions between the input elements.

questions.question: Why is the positional encoding necessary in the Transformer architecture?

options.A: To maintain the order of words in the input sequence
options.C: To add non-linearity in the model
options.D: To enable the model to handle out-of-vocabulary words

questions.explanation: The positional encoding is necessary to maintain the order of words in the input sequence, as the Self-Attention mechanism relies on positions to perform the calculations.

questions.question: What is the name of the Transformer variant that uses relative positions instead of absolute positions?
questions.explanation: The Relative Positional Transformer uses relative positions instead of absolute positions, allowing the model to capture local dependencies in the input sequence.

questions.question: Why is the Residual Connection necessary in the Transformer architecture?

options.A: To prevent the gradients from vanishing or exploding
options.B: To add non-linearity in the model
options.C: To enable the model to handle out-of-vocabulary words
options.D: To maintain the order of words in the input sequence

questions.explanation: The Residual Connection is necessary to prevent the gradients from vanishing or exploding, allowing the model to learn more complex representations of the input sequence.

questions.question: What is the name of the technique used in the Transformer architecture to stabilize the training process?
questions.explanation: Layer Normalization is a technique used to stabilize the training process, as it helps to prevent the gradients from vanishing or exploding.

questions.question: How does the Transformer architecture handle tokenization?
questions.explanation: The Transformer architecture uses subword tokenization, which breaks down words into smaller subword units to handle out-of-vocabulary words.

questions.question: Why is the use of learnable position embeddings necessary in the Transformer architecture?

options.A: To maintain the order of words in the input sequence
options.C: To add non-linearity in the model
options.D: To enable the model to learn the optimal position embeddings

questions.explanation: The use of learnable position embeddings is necessary to enable the model to learn the optimal position embeddings, which is essential for the model to capture long-range dependencies.

questions.question: What is the name of the attention mechanism used in the Transformer architecture to generate the output sequence?
questions.explanation: The Transformer architecture uses all three attention mechanisms mentioned above to generate the output sequence.

questions.question: Why is the use of layer normalization necessary in the Transformer architecture?

options.A: To prevent the gradients from vanishing or exploding
options.B: To add non-linearity in the model
options.D: To enable the model to handle out-of-vocabulary words

questions.explanation: The use of layer normalization is necessary to stabilize the training process, as it helps to prevent the gradients from vanishing or exploding.

questions.question: What is the name of the optimization technique used to reduce the dimensionality of the input sequence?
questions.explanation: The Positional Encoding is used to reduce the dimensionality of the input sequence, allowing the model to focus on more relevant information.

questions.question: Why is the use of the FFNN necessary in the Transformer architecture?

options.A: To add non-linearity in the model
options.D: To enable the model to handle out-of-vocabulary words

questions.explanation: The FFNN is necessary to add non-linearity in the model, allowing it to learn more complex relationships between the input elements.

questions.question: What is the name of the regularization technique used to prevent overfitting in the Transformer architecture?
questions.explanation: The dropout is a regularization technique used to prevent overfitting, as it randomly sets a fraction of the outputs to zero during training.

questions.question: What is the name of the technique used to handle the context window in the Transformer architecture?
questions.explanation: The Look-Ahead Window is used to handle the context window, allowing the model to capture more information about the input sequence.

questions.question: Why is the use of the learnable embedding necessary in the Transformer architecture?

options.A: To maintain the order of words in the input sequence
options.C: To add non-linearity in the model
options.D: To enable the model to learn the optimal embeddings

questions.explanation: The use of the learnable embedding is necessary to enable the model to learn the optimal embeddings, which is essential for the model to handle out-of-vocabulary words.
